claim.statement: In the finite Barrier Atlas decision-core model, no fact row with a missing positive checker/composite/quorum signal can decide to CERTIFIED.
claim.negation_of: a fail-open decision row where checker_positive is false but the decision core returns CERTIFIED
claim.scope: the Lean Facts model in LeanVerificationJourney.RunnerFailClosed, bridged to the Python runner by the committed decision table test

rung.name: kernel-checked decision-core theorem
trusted_base: trusted fact extraction from runner state into the finite Facts model

meta.boundary: Proves the finite decision core cannot return CERTIFIED without the modeled positive signal. It does not prove crypto, key trust, plugin honesty, or the fact-extraction layer.

provenance.pipeline: Lean 4 finite decision-core model; #print axioms audit plus atlas lean-axioms checker; Python bridge compares the exported Lean table to tools/decide.py and representative runner branches

one_directional: The dogfood checker certifies only if the Lean theorem elaborates and its axiom set exactly equals the declared base. Any sorry, extra axiom, failed build, or theorem-name drift fails closed.
